@croos12 Just to double check do you mean 125K or 125M in title?

> @croos12 Just to double check do you mean 125K or 125M in title?

It's 125k total messages per file max. In testing I found that will fit in 32MB for gnmi send/recv buffers
Sorry this is still a bit confusing. I don't see any mentioning of the 125kb in the new code and isn't 125 kb < 4mb? What exactly are you changing to 125kb?

> Sorry this is still a bit confusing. I don't see any mentioning of the 125kb in the new code and isn't 125 kb < 4mb? What exactly are you changing to 125kb?

Sorry these variables are kind of confusing. The "125k" in the title means 125,000 messages (count), not 125 kilobytes. The largest ENI-level object configuration in DASH requires up to 125,000 protobuf messages to be sent in a single gNMI call (CA-to-PA). At that scale, the serialized payload requires a maxMessage size of around ~32MB, which I am setting in my sonic-buildimage PR that requires this change: https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-buildimage/pull/26679

Thanks for the clarification. It would be nice to include some background or pointers to background in the description. 

Also you might want to merge latest master for the pipeline error.
/azp run

Fixed: `go mod tidy` now checks if the installed Go version is >= the go.mod directive before running. Prevents the `maximum version supported by tidy` error on older Go installations.

The TestTranslSubscribe test failures (CRL directory errors) appear pre-existing and unrelated to our change.

Azure Pipelines successfully started running 1 pipeline(s).

@zbud-msft cert rotation will trigger unnecessary restart for UDS server. But current implementation both server are tightly coupled in code so it will be a >100LOC refactor. Given rotation's infrequency and UDS's current application (upgrade) which is also very infrequent. I will do this as a follow up.

Can you check the build failure? Also can you point to the code where DASH_HA tables are written to DB?

> Can you check the build failure? Also can you point to the code where DASH_HA tables are written to DB?

Seems like an unrelated test, will confirm!

For DASH_HA_ tables it will fall into the `else`, and use ProducerStateTable:
https://github.com/zjswhhh/sonic-gnmi/blob/swsscommon_table/sonic_data_client/mixed_db_client.go#L269-L273

Function `DbSetTable` will call this `GetTable` to get the proper producer table and then do the `set` operation.

## Build failure: `generateVersion` redeclared

The `gnmi_server` package fails to compile because `generateVersion` is defined in two test files in the same package:

- `gnsi_certz_test.go:1658`
- `gnsi_pathz_test.go:968`

Go does not allow duplicate top-level declarations in the same package, even across files.

**Fix** — rename one of the two functions to be specific to its domain:

```go
// gnsi_certz_test.go
func generateCertzVersion() string { ... }  // was: generateVersion

// gnsi_pathz_test.go  
func generatePathzVersion() string { ... }  // was: generateVersion
```

Then update the callers in each file to use the renamed function. This is a one-line rename in each file plus updating the call sites within the same file.
